name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash is a national payday and title loan chain with 30 years of operation, offering short-term loans up to $600, check cashing, and financial services at physical store locations.
description_long:
Check Into Cash is one of the oldest and largest short-term lending chains in the United States, having operated for over 30 years according to its own marketing. The company operates licensed brick-and-mortar stores across multiple states, including the Ferndale, Michigan location highlighted here. As a state-licensed lender, it posts its retail fee schedule and state license publicly, operating under Michigan's payday lending regulations.

The company's core product in Michigan is the payday loan, capped at $600, available both in-store and online. Beyond payday loans, Check Into Cash offers a broader suite of short-term lending products including title loans (using a vehicle's clean title as collateral), installment loans (larger amounts repaid over time), and a flex line of credit (a revolving credit facility). Financial services also available at locations include check cashing, bill pay, Western Union money transfers, Green Dot Visa Debit Cards, tax preparation, and money orders.

What distinguishes Check Into Cash from purely online lenders is its physical store model: approved borrowers can walk out of a store with cash in hand the same day. The Ferndale location is open Monday through Saturday with extended hours (8am–7pm on Fridays), providing accessible same-day service. The company has been in operation for three decades, giving it a longer track record than many fintech payday alternatives. Its multi-product model also means a single visit can address check cashing, bill payment, and money transfers simultaneously.

Honestly assessed, Check Into Cash is a high-cost lender of last resort. Payday loans — their primary product — typically carry triple-digit annual percentage rates, and the $600 Michigan cap means they are unsuitable for larger financial needs. Borrowers must have an active checking account, which excludes the fully unbanked from loan products. The company is best used only when lower-cost alternatives (credit unions, employer advances, CDFIs) have been exhausted and the loan can be repaid in full on the next pay date.
pros: 30 years in operation — one of the longest-tenured short-term lenders in the US
Same-day cash disbursement in-store for approved borrowers
Multiple loan products: payday, title, installment, and flex line of credit
Ancillary financial services in one location: check cashing, Western Union, bill pay, tax prep
State-licensed with public retail fee schedule posted at Michigan locations
Extended weekday hours (open until 7pm Mon–Fri) and Saturday availability
Payday loans available both in-store and online in eligible states
cons: Payday loans capped at $600 in Michigan — insufficient for expenses above that threshold
High-cost product — payday loans carry very high APRs not disclosed in the scraped content
Active checking account required for loan applications, excluding fully unbanked consumers
Product availability varies significantly by state — not all services offered at every location
Closed Sundays at this location, limiting access for weekend emergencies
best_for: Consumers needing $600 or less before their next payday with no cheaper credit options available
Borrowers who prefer in-person transactions and need cash in hand the same day
Individuals who need multiple financial services in one visit (loan + check cashing + bill pay)
Consumers in states where Check Into Cash operates who are locked out of traditional bank credit
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best for consumers who need a small emergency loan of $600 or less, have an active checking account, and need cash in hand today from a physical location. The main caveat is cost — payday loans are among the most expensive credit products available, and should only be used when the borrower can repay in full on their next pay date and has exhausted lower-cost alternatives.
services: Online loan applications (availability varies by state)
